reviews.review_text: Fallout New Vegas was the last good fallout game/ anything they decide to do with the name. You can feel the heart and soul of obsidian through their writing and world it's a shame they were rushed but it shows the skill they have with the little time they were given.

On my last playthrough of this game I created a man called Ung. Ung had a strength of 9 and used a club made of rebar and a block of solid concrete to quite literally explode people into meaty giblets. Ung also had both a intelligence of 8 as well as a charisma of 6 and would regularly combat his adversaries in both mental and philosophical debate, saving his comically large rebar club as a last resort.

Ung went on to stop a nuclear disaster with his master debating skills

10/10 God I wish more fallout games were like this one.
Alright so i may have a gambling addiction.. oh also good game must play before you vaporize or something. :)
Honestly the best roleplaying game ive ever played and I loved every second of this work of art.

this has been my favorite game for 15 years, no other game can ever compare. absolute masterpiece. bethesda needs to make more fallout games like this. the newer fallout series games are fun but they just arent the same, they lost their charm :( PLEASE make fallout fun again

Truly one of the best games ever created, from the story to the character designs and interactions it definitely has alot of flaws due to its age but with a few mods ur set, those flaws are bugs and the game being slow cuz running wasnt a mechanic, but if you can get past that you will have the time of ur life story is so good and interesting its hooks u up the freedom as well to make character builds and what not is amazing, the dlcs in this game are also goated so are hard some are easy but they all feel unique and dif from each other completely, if your an open minded person and dont mind old games this is one YOU MUST PLAY

This is a great game. As with other Fallout games, there is so much to do and explore. The Vegas setting was a nice twist. Visiting the strip for the first time back in the day was a super cool experience. I have replayed this game a few times across various systems and I have enjoyed it each time. Although the graphics have aged a bit, the game is still worth picking up and playing to experience the unique characters and story. Also, be sure to check out the mods available for the game; there are some that really polish the experience.

Fun game and all, but i wish i could play the darn thang! Crashes every 10 mins or so and im at the begging, should be illegal to sell the game in this state. Yeah the "just mod it bro" argument can be made, but still i shouldn't have to mod it. remaster the dam thing, it's screaming HELPPPPP!

This game works almost perfectly for me. It crashes every 30 minutes to an hour or so, but that's just how Bethesda games are. This game runs terribly for most people, but I've had no issues. 10/10 game. Highly recommend it as it's one of the greatest RPG games of all time in my opinion. Up in the top 5 for sure

played this game for years, and just recently bought and played it again on PC. this game with the DLCs still lives up to the hype from a decade ago. Best couple bucks spent.

I love fallout new Vegas it give you a excellent apocalyptic setting and a lot unique locations to explore and fun enemies to fight. Skill points even effect the game for example if you make your character dumb your character will give funny/stupid quotes or if you make your character smart it can help you fix or hack objects to make your playthrough easier. The NPC dialog and world lore is amazing. I can't lie i don't think the graphics hold up  today but i think the game graphics are ok enough for today standards but that's just my opinion. Even though fallout new Vegas is a old game i think its still great game but there some problems that might turn away from playing which include bugs/crashing, Warning when i last tried to play the game wouldn't launch so you might want to research it first before playing.

"Groundbreaking for 2010, but i'm not playing it in 2010."

story is the best in the series considering quality across the board. my opinion obviously. 

lil buggy to this day lmao.

This game has a surprisingly insightful commentary on the two party political system and how one team is comically evil and obviously no one wants them in charge, while the other team is well-meaning, but is too distant from the people they're trying to rule over that they can't really be anything more than a band aid solution to a bullet wound. And while those two parties are fighting the real rich guy in charge is paying you to keep the status quo going so that he can remain in control. But then, there's the secret third option (which is arguably the least developed of the four which is kind of sad because I love Yes-Man. More Yes-Man please) which I like to imagine is the metaphorical third party. It's really cool and very relevant to our modern political situation.
On top of that, this game has AMAZING RPG mechanics. Highly recommend playing with 10 charisma and 1 intelligence, it'll make for some riveting gameplay.
NOT TO MENTION!!! The companions in this game are spectacular. I always struggle to choose who I want by my side because I love all of them so much. And the fact that you can all live together in one big apartment is AMAZING. Probably my favorite found family of all time is in this game. Also you can take Rex (the goodest boy of all good boys) with you while travelling with other companions. It's great. Small tip tho: Save before finishing Arcade's quest. I know, it's tempting, because he wants to tell you his sad backstory so bad it makes him look stupid. But once you finish his questline you'll never see him again. So either wait until you complete the game to finish it or save and reload so you can have him back.
Overall an amazing game. Despite it's age, it's very fun and quickly wormed it's way into my heart. 10/10, would recommend. 
...
Also YOU CAN ♥♥♥♥ BENNY GHECKO!?

Fallout New Vegas is an awesome RPG experience inside a fun looter/shooter (and survival on Hardcore mode) with infinite replayability especially with an amazing modding community. Get it on special with the DLC's mod it up and start it up have fun lol.

Fallout New Vegas is one of the most expansive and greatest RPGs of all time. A rich post apocalyptic world filled with dozens of unique factions, quests, and enemies. The level of choice and consequences is unmatched compared to Fallout 3 & 4. You feel like your decisions matter and hold weight. The game is still has tech issues, highly recommend installing Viva New Vegas mod pack for the optimal performance experience

Despite the rumors of its power, this game did NOT make me transgender, but it DID make me appreciate the power of strong narratives in games. The gameplay can be complete dogwater at times, sure, but when the writing is THIS good - with sidequests that almost act as short stories unto themselves - none of that matters. In this case, the journey matters a hell of a lot more than the destination.

Simply one of the best RPG games ever made. If you love Roleplaying games of any kind, this game is a must-have, no doubt - it has its issues, mostly related to how clunky the engine is sometimes, but it's more than compensated by its amazing story and progression.

Inspired by the many references in the Amazon series (actually this game is more like the BASE for the series) I picked this up again after finishing the main storyline many years ago.

And OH MY GOD, I didn't realize it at the time but BOY this is a masterpiece. Obsidian's writing and quality-over-quantity approach elevates the whole franchise. Such a coherent game. Both story and gameplay. Came back to just do the DLCs and now I am convinced that without this game and its narrative & worldbuilding, the TV series would  not have happened^^ It's just a timeless classic.

PS: Runs smoothly in 4k on my fairly modern Windows 11 machine.

What i love the most about FNV how it does two things at the same time. First it does accurately capture the landscape of the Mohave and the Southwest US. But it also has its own atmosphere and feel and I think that is what attracts new players and keeps old players returning.

This has always been one of my favorite Fallout games and it's so cool to be able to take it on the go with the Steam Deck. The audio can sometimes cut out but close the game and reopen it and it solves the issue right away. I love the compatibility with the steam deck because the controls just fit perfectly for it. Strongly suggest grabbing this title when it goes on sale to grab all the DLC but highly recommend the game for sure with the Steam Deck.

As an older game, Fallout NV definitely has bugs. Although, many of these can be patched effectively via anti-crash mods. As far as gameplay goes, the combat is fun: enemies vary and have different weaknesses, you are able to choose your own fighting style, whether that be long-ranged and stealthy, up-close and powerful, or agile with rapid-fire, you can develop your skills and abilities to your liking as you go. The recruitable companions are all pretty beneficial, and I enjoyed their corresponding quests. Personally, I found the atmosphere itself to be pretty appealing, and I had a thoroughly pleasant time exploring the different regions of the map. The desert has a certain empty and dismal vastness to it. There are many dilapidated ruins of an old world scattered throughout, with secrets you can poke your nose into, and hostiles to keep you alert and on your toes. The main storyline is engaging and in-depth, with your choices carving out the course of the plot and heavily impacting the fate of the Mojave. The side quests can provide you with considerable benefits and loot, and many of the characters you will meet are intriguing and have some decently humorous dialogue. Lastly, I would recommend all of the DLC (notably, Old World Blues, Lonesome Road, and Honest Hearts) for amusing extended content and powerful gear. But yeah, this game is classic.

Started with fallout 4 and enjoyed it a lot except for not being able to kill Preston and other annoying npcs. This game is awesome for allowing you to do whatever you want. I did have a few game crashes on ultra despite having good PC specs. Changed it to Medium and have not had any yet.

[h2] If you love RPG's with choices, then you have to play New Vegas [/h2]

I adore this game. Don't worry about the other titles, just start with this one.

Everything about it is so well written, that you won't even care that it's from 2010. As a kid i always loved fallout 3 and 4, but as an adult it's easy to see just how much better Fallout New Vegas is. It references things from fallout 1 and 2, and in many ways feels like more of a continuation of the story than the fallout 3 we got. Bethesda isn't always great with the lore and RPG decisions they make for fallout, and there's a chance this might be the last fallout game that truly understands the universe.

There's so many choices to make, so many awesome quests, great game design and you can replay New Vegas many, many times. Even though the game crashes and has a lot of creation engine jank, it get's hard carried by it's writing so much that it actually holds up. 

[b] I love the fallout universe, and so should you. Get the ultimate edition for dirt cheap on a sale and have fun! [/b]


[i] On a sidenote: The Amazon Fallout TV-series continues the story and you'll enjoy it A LOT more if you've played New Vegas. [/i]

never ending things to find the more times i replay the more i find and the more stuff i learn about the story and more ways to walk through the game and jump around to do crazy stuff and get stuff fast
Fun story that allows you to make nuanced choices. Has great mod support. Improvement over the foundation Fallout 3 built.

👍 Recommended

Fallout: New Vegas isn't just the best Fallout game—it's one of the best RPGs ever made.

The Mojave Wasteland feels alive in a way few open-world games manage. Every faction has believable goals, every major choice has consequences, and the game trusts you to decide what kind of person your Courier becomes. Whether you're backing the NCR, joining Caesar's Legion, helping Mr. House, or taking control for yourself, the story adapts around your decisions instead of forcing you down a single path.

The writing is exceptional. Quests rarely boil down to simple "good" or "evil" choices, and many of the most memorable moments come from conversations rather than combat. The DLCs are equally fantastic, especially Old World Blues and Lonesome Road, which expand the world and themes in unique ways.

Sure, it's old. The graphics show their age, the combat can feel clunky, and the game occasionally reminds you that it was built on a mountain of duct tape and hope. But none of that takes away from what makes New Vegas special.

15 years later, people are still arguing about factions, replaying different builds, and discovering new quest outcomes. That's the mark of a legendary RPG.

10/10

Patrolling the Mojave almost makes you wish for a nuclear winter.

Even though people say this game is 'overrated' I personally love this game, one thing I recommend is whenever you start a new game or go to your current saved game ALWAYS turn on radio New Vegas, trust me, that radio not only has the best and absolute bangers in it, the radio also gives the game the vibe and will always make the game a little more fun, and one more thing, I would recommend getting the lonesome rode DLC, one your a high enough level you can get the elite NCR ranger armor, but if you go in at a low level, would recommend selling everything inside the DLC map so you could get the armor, but in conclusion, I would give this game a 9/10, would recommend and will always be a classic.

[h1]Deathclaws are on PCP and Speed in this.[/h1]

At first glance, yes, the game is clunky, yes, you can accidentally clip into a rock and not be able to fast travel out of it because it says you're falling. Yet, besides it's minor mechanical flaws, this is truly a phenomenal game. I loved Fallout 3 in its entirety and this expands on where that game fell short. Not to say that Fallout 3 was bad because it was so unique and incredibly entertaining. Fallout: NV makes my spurs jingle-jangle. A game worth your time. It's more challenging than Fallout 4, where-as at level 10 in F4 you can most-likely take down a Deathclaw.
For FNV, good-effing-luck trying to even run away fast enough. You will poop your pants at the first sound of their grunts as they charge you. At level 10, with metal armor and a grenade launcher, you'll most likely succumb to their death-lunge. 10/10

says 74h played but iv played well over 3k total on xbox and pc iv been all over even after all this time i still find new things from time to time 10/10  its buggy and alittle rough around the edges but its a fantastic game

I inhaled this game when it came out. I'm not much of an Elder Scrolls guy, but this is my favorite game in this engine by far. Finally, a main story that feels natural to AVOID. "That guy shot me in the head. I kinda don't want to talk to him right now. What else is going on out there?"

Or you can make a beeline to him and power-fist him into mashed potatoes as your first order of business.

Fallout: New Vegas is a 10/10 and simply the best game in the Fallout series. The writing, factions, choices, atmosphere, and RPG freedom are unmatched. Every playthrough feels different. The Mojave Wasteland is incredibly immersive, and the DLCs make the game even better, especially Old World Blues and Dead Money.

I mainly played through FitGirl Repacks to get all the DLCs together, so my hours and achievements don’t really show how much I’ve played. Even without tracked stats, I’ve spent countless hours exploring, replaying endings, and trying different builds. New Vegas is still one of the greatest RPGs ever made and the Fallout game I always come back to
